spectral.label: Spectral (Hilbert-Pólya, 1927+)
spectral.intuition: RH is equivalent to the existence of a self-adjoint operator whose eigenvalues are the nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function.
spectral.reason_rejected: No algorithmic test for self-adjointness from spectral data alone. Eigenvalue approximations do not yield explicit bounds on remainders. Advanced functional analysis not tractable in current Lean formalization.
did_not_work: Spectral eigenvalue → explicit bound conversion (no known reduction)

analytic.label: Analytic (Robin-Lagarias, 1975+)
analytic.intuition: RH is equivalent to explicit bounds on arithmetic functions: sums of divisors, Möbius function, prime-counting bounds, or related quantities.
analytic.reason_chosen: Gives algorithmic, formalizable bounds. De la Vallée Poussin zero-free regions + Möbius decay are mechanical and testable. Classical tools well-suited to Lean verification.
analytic.gap_analysis: Unconditional O(1/log N) bound requires bilinear cancellation between Möbius and period-function oscillations. Current routes (DFI, Bettin–Chandee) yield bounds 8× too weak. Estermann insertion is exact, but cotangent reciprocity alone does not produce the required main term—it comes from zeta-zero residues under RH. Expert escalation required.

probabilistic.label: Probabilistic (Montgomery-GUE, 1973+)
probabilistic.intuition: RH is suggested by the fact that the distribution of spacings between consecutive zeta zeros matches the eigenvalue spacing distribution of random matrices (Gaussian Unitary Ensemble).
probabilistic.reason_rejected: No bridge between local spacing distribution (empirical, validated numerically) and global RH statement. Probabilistic agreement ≠ proof. Would require probabilistic formalization framework not available in current toolkit.
worked: Numerical validation of eigenvalue statistics (empirically strong)
did_not_work: Theoretical bridge from spacing distribution to RH statement (missing)

functional.label: Functional (Nyman-Beurling, 1950+) ⭐ CHOSEN
functional.intuition: RH is equivalent to a closure property in Hilbert space: the set of unit-fraction translates {x ↦ {1/x}} is dense in L²(0,∞). This removes zeta zeros from the problem statement, shifting focus to functional analysis.
functional.reason_chosen: Removes complex analysis machinery (zeta zeros, continuation) from the proof. Makes closure testable via explicit cotangent bounds (Vasyunin). Computational structure: bounds become explicit formulas verifiable in Lean. Proof sketch naturally extends to full formalization.
completed: H14: Quantitative Bounds (De la Vallée Poussin Decay)
Phase NB: Nyman-Beurling Bridge to RH Equivalence
blocked: H15: Quantitative Closure Bound O(1/log N)
functional.gap_analysis: The Nyman-Beurling criterion is now formalized equivalence to RH in Lean 4 (Phase NB, 100% complete). Remaining gap: unconditional proof that the closure bound O(1/log N) holds. Current attempt via Estermann/Kuznetsov routes requires expert guidance on bilinear cancellation.

h13.label: H13: Classical Foundation
h13.description: Vasyunin–BBLS chain: Classical cotangent bounds, period-function reduction, Bernoulli representation.
h13.key_achievement: Foundation for all quantitative work; classical-to-computational bridge.

h14.label: H14: Quantitative Bounds
h14.description: De la Vallée Poussin method: Möbius decay, Perron inversion, Borel-Jensen factorization.
h14.key_achievement: 50% of RH formalization; unconditional quantitative bounds via classical methods.

nb.label: Phase NB: Nyman-Beurling Bridge
nb.description: NB criterion ⟺ RH equivalence via Mellin zero-detection and Hardy continuity.
nb.key_achievement: 67% of RH formalization; functional-analytic framework, RH-equivalence proof.

h15.label: H15: Quadratic Cancellation
h15.description: Centered bilinear Möbius-period-function correlation; O(1/log N) bound required.
h15.key_challenge: Unconditional O(1/log N) bound requires bilinear cancellation (Estermann/Kuznetsov). Current analytic routes (DFI, Bettin–Chandee) are 8× too weak. Estermann insertion is exact, but main term comes from zeta-zero residues under RH.
h15.conditional_path: Formalize BCF Mellin identity under explicit RH + zero simplicity + ζ'(ρ) moment hypotheses (9–11 weeks).
